sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, an 82-year-old male, presents for hospital follow-up after an acute on chronic congestive heart failure exacerbation.
He reports being on a diet to manage his condition but has had dietary indiscretions, including consuming pizza and chicken wings, which led to swelling of his ankles and shortness of breath.
He was hospitalized for two days, during which time he was treated with a medication that increased urination and a water pill given intravenously.
He reports feeling significantly better since discharge and has been adhering to his medication regimen and diet.
He has also purchased a blood pressure cuff and has been monitoring his blood pressure, which has been good.
He denies any shortness of breath, chest pain, or problems sleeping since discharge.
Past medical history: The patient has a past medical history significant for hypertension and stage three chronic kidney disease.
Review of systems: The patient denies any additional symptoms in other organs relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: I prescribed bumex 2mg daily for congestive heart failure.
He is also taking cozaar 100mg daily and norvasc 5mg daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 128/72, heart rate is 79, respiratory rate is 26, and oxygen saturation is 99%.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is unremarkable, with no jugular venous distention.
The heart exam reveals a stable, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The lungs are clear bilaterally, and the lower extremities show a trace of edema.
Test Results: The patient's echocardiogram shows a preserved ejection fraction of 55%, abnormal diastolic filling, and mild to moderate mitral regurgitation.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 82-year-old male with congestive heart failure, hypertension, and stage three chronic kidney disease.
Problem list: Congestive heart failure; Hypertension; Stage three chronic kidney disease; Mild to moderate mitral regurgitation.
Plan:
I want the patient to continue taking bumex 2mg daily for congestive heart failure and to watch his diet, avoiding salty foods, including pizza and chicken wings.
I also want him to weigh himself daily and call me if he gains three pounds in two days.
I will order a renal artery ultrasound to rule out secondary causes of his kidney disease.
I will also refer him to a nutritionist for education on managing his diet.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in three months.
